behavior_in_task_description.reason: Every test checks file paths (e.g., java/src/org/openqa/selenium/io/Read.java), method signatures (resourceAsString(Class<?>, String)), class names (CdpEventTypes.class, Read.class), and patterns (getClass(), @Deprecated) that are all explicitly described in instruction.md's Required Changes section.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md section 'Required Changes' gives the exact solution: the exact new method signature, that the old method must delegate with Read.class, and exactly which classes must call with which arguments (e.g., 'CdpEventTypes.java must call Read.resourceAsString(CdpEventTypes.class, ...)'). This is not just describing what's broken—it's prescribing the exact fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests use regex patterns matching specific implementation details like 'Read.resourceAsString(CdpEventTypes.class,' and 'Read.resourceAsString(getClass(),' and the exact delegation pattern 'resourceAsString(Read.class'. An alternative correct fix (e.g., using Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader(), or a different parameter name/order, or a caller-stack-walking approach) would fail these tests even if it correctly solves the OSGi problem.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test (both f2p and p2p) uses only regex/string matching on source file contents (e.g., re.search(pattern, content)). No test compiles, executes, or imports any Java code. This is purely textual pattern matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check for patterns that genuinely don't exist in the base commit (e.g., the new two-argument method signature, @Deprecated annotation, delegation to Read.class, caller updates). A stub/no-op wouldn't pass because the base files don't contain these patterns.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/selenium's solve.sh (solution/solve.sh), and the agent could read it. Additionally, test_outputs.py is in tests/ which the agent could potentially access. The regex patterns in tests are simple enough that the agent could grep for expected patterns and insert them mechanically.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five required changes from instruction.md are tested: new method signature, @Deprecated annotation, delegation pattern, and all four caller updates (CdpEventTypes, RemoteScript, W3CHttpCommandCodec, ReadTest).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped into 'FAIL_TO_PASS' and 'PASS_TO_PASS' sections with clear comments, and each test has a descriptive name and docstring explaining what it verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves Java source code modifications, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, class names, method signatures, and variable names in instruction.md match the actual repository structure and test expectations.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the exact method signatures to create, exact classes to modify, and exact calling patterns (e.g., 'CdpEventTypes.java must call Read.resourceAsString(CdpEventTypes.class, ...)'). This goes far beyond identifying the buggy file—it prescribes the exact code changes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task involving Java source file modifications, not configuration file edits.
